name: Three Rivers Community Action, Inc
description_short: Non-profit community action agency providing free and low-cost assistance with housing, energy, food, and support services across southern Minnesota since 1966.
description_long:
Three Rivers Community Action, Inc. is a nonprofit human service organization incorporated in 1966 and created by local citizens to address basic human needs in their service area. The organization operates under a mission to work with community partners to improve quality of life for individuals, families, and the community. They receive funding from Minnesota Community Action Grants and Community Services Block Grants administered through the Office of Economic Opportunity.

Three Rivers offers a comprehensive suite of assistance programs spanning housing, nutrition, energy, childcare, and support services for people at different life stages. Their core offerings include rental assistance, energy assistance, weatherization, mobile home rehabilitation, SNAP enrollment support, meals on wheels, chore and homemaker services for seniors, Head Start childcare, volunteer transportation, housing advocacy, homelessness support through McKinney-Vento assistance, and homeownership education through their Home Stretch Workshop. They operate multiple physical locations across Goodhue, Wabasha, Rice, and Steele Counties in Minnesota.

Three Rivers distinguishes itself through its multi-stage life approach, serving renters, homeowners, families, seniors, and people experiencing homelessness with tailored programs. They provide culturally responsive services in English, Somali, and Spanish. Their chore and homemaker services specifically target adults 65+ seeking to remain independent in their homes. The organization maintains an explicit commitment to cost-sharing based on ability to pay, with a stated policy that no one will be turned away for inability to contribute.

As a legitimate nonprofit dependent on public grants, Three Rivers represents genuine community assistance rather than a commercial service. The limitation is that services are geographically restricted to specific Minnesota counties and availability varies by location. Funding constraints mean some services like chore assistance are currently operational in only "selected communities." The organization is transparent about its grant funding sources and executive leadership.
pros: Non-profit status ensures services prioritize need over profit; funded by government Community Action Grants
Multilingual services available in English, Somali, and Spanish reflecting diverse community needs
Cost-sharing model explicitly states no one will be turned away for inability to pay
Comprehensive wraparound services addressing multiple needs: housing, food, energy, childcare, senior support
Established 1966 with proven track record and community partnerships across multiple counties
Homeownership education through Home Stretch Workshop helps renters transition to ownership
Senior-focused chore and homemaker services help adults 65+ maintain independence in their homes
cons: Services limited to specific geographic areas in southern Minnesota (Goodhue, Wabasha, Rice, Steele Counties)
Some services like chore and homemaker assistance are only available in 'selected communities,' not universally across service area
Limited information on website about wait times, service capacity, or how to formally apply for assistance
Dependent on government grant funding which may fluctuate and affect service availability or expansion
No online application or intake process documented; appears to require in-person visits to offices
best_for: Low-income renters and families in southern Minnesota seeking rental or energy assistance
Adults 65+ in Goodhue and Wabasha Counties needing in-home support to maintain independence
Individuals and families experiencing homelessness or housing instability in their service area
Families with young children seeking Head Start enrollment and childcare support
diagnosis: Three Rivers Community Action is best for low-to-moderate income individuals and families in southern Minnesota seeking free or low-cost assistance with housing, food, energy, or senior support services. The main caveat is that services are geographically restricted to specific Minnesota counties and some programs operate only in selected communities within that region, requiring verification of local availability before applying.
services: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) enrollment and support
Chore and Homemaker Services for adults 65+
Housing Advocacy and homeless support through McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act
Head Start early childhood education programs at multiple locations
Homeownership education and Home Stretch Workshop classes
Community Support Services for people experiencing homelessness
Volunteer opportunities and community engagement programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1926 College View Rd E, Suite 1302, Rochester, MN 55904
